findings.title: Launchable fork gate makes the default Foundry suite depend on live Base RPC
findings.why_it_matters: Once the manifest is launchable, `test_BaseForkLaunchGateRequiresPinnedBlockAndRpc` reverts when `BASE_RPC` is missing, while the rest of the fork tests use `vm.skip(true)`. That turns ordinary `forge test` runs without Doppler/RPC into failures instead of keeping pure tests and live fork validation separated.
evidence: Line 84 reads `BASE_RPC`; lines 85-86 revert if it is empty after launchability is true.
The helper used by the rest of the fork suite skips when `BASE_RPC` is absent, so this one test is the outlier gate.
findings.suggested_fix: Move the hard BASE_RPC requirement behind an explicit launch/live validation test target or profile, and keep the default Foundry suite skip-based when RPC is absent. Add a separate documented command or CI job that requires Doppler/Base RPC and runs the pinned fork gate.

findings.title: Deployment validator mismatch branches are not covered
findings.why_it_matters: The new validation script is a deployment gate, but tests only cover a happy router config plus venue target, skip-NFT, and snapshot failures. The fee recipient, fee ppm, owner, chain id, and live metadata mismatch branches can regress without a test failing.
evidence: `validateRouterConfiguration` has explicit revert branches for chain id, fee recipient, fee ppm, and owner at script/ValidateFameRouterBase.s.sol:77.

`validateLivePoolMetadata` has token, factory, identity, stable, tick-spacing, and fee mismatch branches at script/ValidateFameRouterBase.s.sol:158, but the suite only calls the happy path on a pinned fork.
findings.suggested_fix: Add targeted negative tests for wrong `BASE_CHAIN_ID`, `BASE_FAME_ROUTER_FEE_RECIPIENT`, `BASE_FAME_ROUTER_FEE_PPM`, and `BASE_FAME_ROUTER_OWNER`. For live metadata, add a small mock/harness path or fixture override that exercises token, factory, stable, fee, and tick-spacing mismatch selectors without depending on mutating the Base fork.

findings.title: Several adapter encoding tests only assert that structured payloads are hex
findings.why_it_matters: The route artifacts depend on exact ABI shapes, but the Solidly and Slipstream tests would still pass if field order, deadline, stable flag, tick spacing, or router/factory encoding regressed. Fork tests may be skipped without RPC, so pure tests need to catch these schema mistakes.
evidence: The Solidly test at line 28 only checks `payload.startsWith("0x")`.
The Slipstream test at line 33 only checks `payload.startsWith("0x")` despite the adapter encoding seven typed fields.
findings.suggested_fix: Decode each payload with the expected ABI and assert every behaviorally relevant field. Do the same for Universal Router V3/V4 structured payload tests where they currently rely on hex-shape assertions rather than decoded recipient, payer, amount, pool key, and hook-data fields.

residual_risks: Pinned Base route execution still depends on external RPC state and should remain a separately required live-validation step for release.
Aerodrome V2 has one launchable USDC->WETH fixture path; broader route quality and reverse-direction selection remain outside this contracts test suite unless future supported fixtures add them.
testing_gaps: Default-vs-live fork test separation for launchable Base validation.
Negative deployment validation for router config and pool metadata mismatch selectors.
Decoded ABI assertions for TypeScript adapter payloads instead of hex-shape checks.
